summary: Iteration 60 CRITICAL FIX RE-VERIFIED. Both server.py code paths (fresh-create @ L5346 and reactivate-deleted @ L5236) now correctly persist office_location. Backend pytest suite is 13/13 PASS including the newly-added reactivate-branch test. Migration invariants intact: 53 active Besant Nagar - Chennai, exactly 1 Inactive 'Sathish E' with blank office_location, zero active employees with blank office_location. Frontend Add + Edit dialogs both show red asterisk, dropdown has ONLY ['Besant Nagar - Chennai', 'Mandaveli - Chennai'] (NO Unassigned), Edit dialog pre-selects the persisted value. Partial PUT (without office_location key) correctly preserves the existing value. Regression clean — no new console errors on Employees page, filters/list still load.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L5346: FIX CONFIRMED — Employee(...) constructor now includes `office_location=data.office_location`. Fresh-create path verified via pytest test_create_with_valid_office_location_succeeds (POST → GET returns 'Besant Nagar - Chennai').

server.py L5236: FIX CONFIRMED — reactivate-deleted update_data dict now includes `"office_location": data.office_location`. Verified via new test_reactivate_persists_new_office_location: created employee with 'Besant Nagar - Chennai', flipped is_deleted=True in Mongo, POSTed with same email + 'Mandaveli - Chennai', GET returned 'Mandaveli - Chennai'.

server.py L5490-5492: update_employee validation correctly rejects only explicit blank/whitespace office_location. Partial PUT without the key preserves existing value (verified via live call on employee 72bcb11b - OL stayed 'Mandaveli - Chennai').

Employees.js L1555 (Add) & L1782 (Edit): both dialogs render `Office Location *` — verified via live DOM inspection. Both share data-testid='office-location-select'. Dropdown options fetched from SSOT /api/settings/office-locations return exactly ['Besant Nagar - Chennai', 'Mandaveli - Chennai'] — 'Unassigned' string does not appear in either dropdown.

Note: the reactivate branch (server.py L5205-5319) is essentially dead code in typical UX flow because the DELETE /api/employees/{id} endpoint (L5571) performs a soft deactivate (sets employee_status=Inactive) — NOT is_deleted=True. Only permanent/force-delete-then-recreate scenarios would hit that branch. Fix is still correct and defensive.

success_rate.backend: 13/13 (100%) — all iteration-59 tests + new reactivate-branch test pass
success_rate.frontend: 100% — asterisk + no-Unassigned + edit-dialog pre-selection + list-load verified

test_credentials: HR Admin: admin / HrAdmin786$ (PINNED, verified via localhost:8001 — preview URL works too but ~40s login latency due to bcrypt)
seed_data_creation: None retained — each test suite run creates 1-2 TEST employees and cleans them up (soft-delete). One test uses direct Mongo update to flip is_deleted=True (documented in test docstring). DB state restored after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both server.py branches for office_location persistence are now correct. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_office_location_required.py (13/13). NOTE: reactivate branch test uses pymongo directly to set is_deleted=True since regular DELETE endpoint uses soft-deactivate (employee_status=Inactive, is_deleted remains False). The reactivate branch is only reachable via a manual is_deleted=True state — worth noting to main agent if they wish to add an actual endpoint that sets is_deleted=True.
rca_of_the_issue: RESOLVED. The iter59 defect ('POST /api/employees drops office_location') is fixed at both L5346 (fresh-create Employee(...) constructor) and L5236 (reactivate update_data dict). Confirmed via automated pytest that POST with office_location='Besant Nagar - Chennai' followed by GET returns 'Besant Nagar - Chennai' (previously returned '').
